blocked.reason: Plan reviewed over 4 rounds; all findings addressed per convergence rule. Remaining 'repairable' state is infrastructure (partial-coverage, SHA ceremony). Tasks.md authored. Same decision as orchestrator.

questions.question: Scope: fix both schema gaps (fields that exist in resolvers but not in the schema) AND convert JSON-scalar types (cluster analysis output) into proper GraphQL object types, or only the schema gaps?
questions.recommendation: Schema gaps only. Leave JSON-scalar types alone.
questions.rationale: The two problems have very different shapes. Schema gaps are ~10 missing fields across 3 existing types — add field, regen, delete manual type. JSON-scalar conversion means defining ~6 new GraphQL object types (ClusterMember, DomainCluster, ValueFaultLine, ClusterPairFaultLines, ClusterAnalysis, DomainAnalysisResult), updating every resolver that returns cluster analysis to construct the nested object graph instead of returning a JSON blob, and dealing with backward compatibility on cached AnalysisResult.output rows. That's a different project. The file's own comment already marks the split line ('kept manual — JSON scalars'). We follow that line.

questions.question: Orphan mutation: the frontend has a hand-written 'BACKFILL_DOMAIN_EVALUATION_MODELS_MUTATION' using raw gql template because the mutation isn't in the schema. Include it in this feature (add Pothos mutation), or defer?
questions.recommendation: Include it. Add the mutation to the schema via Pothos, regen, delete the manual gql block.
questions.rationale: It's two files of change (mutation resolver + regen), lives in the same area, and the frontend already calls it via its own document. The resolver logic presumably already exists somewhere (since something is processing this mutation end-to-end) — we just need to wire it into the Pothos schema. If instead it turns out the resolver doesn't exist and the frontend is dispatching a broken mutation, that's something we want to know right now, not later. Small risk of scope creep.

questions.question: Verification: how do we know we removed everything we intended to remove? Linter rule, test assertion, manual checklist, or just 'grep for exported types at PR time'?
questions.recommendation: Grep-based assertion in the PR description plus a simple 'these types should not be exported after the change' list in tasks.md. No linter rule.
questions.rationale: A custom ESLint rule or AST guard to enforce 'never hand-type these shapes again' is overkill for a one-time cleanup. The JSON-scalar manual types are staying (Q1), so a blanket rule is wrong anyway. A grep-based checklist keeps the scope tight: before-count / after-count of hand-typed exports in the two files, plus an explicit list of symbols that must be removed (Domain, DomainEvaluationMember, DomainEvaluation in domains.ts; plus the orphan mutation document). Easy to verify in review, no long-term tool overhead.

discovery.summary: Replace hand-maintained frontend types in domainAnalysis.ts and domains.ts with generated GraphQL types. Two failure modes coexist: (a) true JSON-scalar fields the schema can't describe (cluster analysis output), (b) fields the resolvers return but the GraphQL schema never declared (defaultModelIds, modelIds, launchableDefinitions, etc.). Three questions: scope of fix, treatment of orphan mutation, verification approach.

answers.Scope: fix both schema gaps (fields that: Schema gaps only. JSON-scalar conversion is a separate, deferred feature (finding-2b-json-scalar-graphql-objects). Both are worth doing, but they have different shapes and review needs.
answers.Orphan mutation: the frontend has a hand: Include it. Add the mutation to the schema via Pothos (wire up existing handler if present, or implement if missing), regen, delete the manual gql block.
answers.Verification: how do we know we removed: Two layers: (a) grep-based before/after list in PR description, plus explicit 'these symbols must not exist after the change' list in tasks.md; (b) custom ESLint rule that fails CI when a new hand-typed GraphQL shape is added in cloud/apps/web/src/api/operations/, with an allowlist for the JSON-scalar types in domainAnalysis.ts that are intentionally staying. No CLAUDE.md note.

non_goals: JSON-scalar conversion: defining GraphQL object types for ClusterMember, DomainCluster, ValueFaultLine, ClusterPairFaultLines, ClusterAnalysis, DomainAnalysisResult; migrating cached AnalysisResult.output rows; rewriting resolvers that return cluster analysis. Tracked separately as a future feature.
